title: Lasianthus mucronulatus
taxonomy: Lasianthus mucronulatus is a species of flowering plant in the family Theaceae, commonly known as the tea family. It belongs to the genus Lasianthus, which consists of various species of evergreen shrubs. Lasianthus mucronulatus is native to the tropical forests of Asia, particularly in countries such as India, Sri Lanka, and China. This plant is classified as a member of the order Ericales, which includes other flowering plants like heath and blueberry. Further classification includes family Theaceae, genus Lasianthus, and species L. mucronulatus.

intro: Lasianthus mucronulatus, also known as the Lasianthus shrub, is a small, evergreen shrub native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Theaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Lasianthus mucronulatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit significant pharmacological activities. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory and digestive issues.
